---
title: "Full run — results"
---

::: {.callout-note}
Numbers on this page are computed at render time from
`output/full/compare/summary.csv` and the coadd `.asdf` files, so a
re-rendered report picks up any re-run results.
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from pathlib import Path

REPO = Path("../..").resolve()
df = pd.read_csv(REPO / "output/full/compare/summary.csv")
```

## Configuration

324 sims over 6 visits (2 passes × 3 dithers × segments 490 + 521),
320 skycells after `skycell_asn --product-type full`.

## Depth distribution

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of per-skycell depth metrics across the full run. Left: the maximum per-pixel depth reached in each skycell. Right: the median per-pixel depth (a proxy for how well the skycell is covered overall)."
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4))
for ax, col in zip(axes, ["max_depth", "median_depth"]):
    counts = df[col].value_counts().sort_index()
    ax.bar(counts.index, counts.values, edgecolor="k", lw=0.5)
    ax.set_xlabel(col)
    ax.set_ylabel("# skycells")
    ax.set_xticks(sorted(df[col].unique()))
    ax.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.3)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Headline numbers

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Headline recovery numbers, medians across all 320 skycells."
summary_tbl = pd.DataFrame({
    "metric": [
        "50%-completeness mag (all sources)",
        "50%-completeness mag (deep subset)",
        "overall completeness (all sources)",
        "overall completeness (deep subset)",
        "Kron flux residual (median, matched)",
        "astrometric residual (median, matched)",
    ],
    "median": [
        f"{df['mag_50pct_all'].median():.2f}",
        f"{df['mag_50pct_deep'].median():.2f}",
        f"{df['completeness_all'].median():.3f}",
        f"{df['completeness_deep'].median():.3f}",
        f"{df['median_dmag_all'].median():+.3f} mag",
        f"{df['median_dpos_all'].median():.3f}″",
    ],
})
summary_tbl
```

## Per-skycell figure gallery

Showing the 60 skycells with the largest `mean_depth` (deepest coverage),
sorted deepest-first. Click any thumbnail to zoom.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
top = df.sort_values("mean_depth", ascending=False).head(60)
print("::: {layout-ncol=3}\n")
for _, row in top.iterrows():
    name = row["skycell"]
    cap = (f"{name} — "
           f"mean_depth={row['mean_depth']:.2f}, "
           f"mag50={row['mag_50pct_all']:.2f}")
    print(f"![{cap}](_plots/{name}.png)\n\n")
print(":::")
```

Each figure has five panels (completeness vs input mag, flux residual vs
input mag with the deep subset highlighted, PSF-vs-aperture magnitude, FP
fraction vs recovered mag, astrometric residual vs input mag) plus a text
summary panel with headline numbers for that skycell.

## Aggregated depth histogram

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Per-pixel depth aggregated across every valid pixel of every skycell. Produced by stage 06 as `depth_distribution.png`; inlined here for reference."
```

![](_depth_distribution.png){fig-alt="Per-pixel depth histogram"}
